$comment: Shipped <sys/types.h> POSIX typedefs + the FIRST subdirectory descriptor (sys/types.json). off_t/ssize_t/time_t -> i64, mode_t -> u32; exit 42 (30+2+10+0) witnesses the subdir resolution + typedef injection on the unix ABIs (elf x86-64 + arm64-qemu + macho). time_t is re-provided by sys/types.json (glibc <sys/types.h> pulls it via <bits/types/time_t.h>) and this TU does NOT include <time.h>, so time_t here is a SECOND red-on-disable edge (drop it from the descriptor -> `time_t when` fails S0006, no binary). NO windows-pe arm: <sys/types.h> is a POSIX header now declared `availableObjectFormats:[elf,macho]`, so a pe compile FAILS LOUD (c8 per-target availability) — proven by the dedicated shipped_header_unavailable_pe error example. RED-on-disable: without the resolveSystemDescriptor subdir-preserving fix, <sys/types.h> maps to a non-existent types.json and the header fails to resolve -> compile fails. The release arm confirms the full shipped pipeline.
